response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior engineer with strong fundamentals in testing, distributed systems exposure, and notable geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map work. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as primary skills, and the resume shows only brief C/C++ exposure from 2014-2015 at National Instruments with no Rust experience. Recent work is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. The geospatial background and willingness to learn beyond current expertise are positives, but the core language gap is significant for a routing tiling role focused on performance-critical pipelines.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Strong testing discipline (TDD, fluent-asserts library, workshops)
Low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic with async I/O and high-throughput services
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only 6 months in 2014-2015) and no Rust
Recent stack is web/TypeScript-focused, not systems programming
No demonstrated experience with routing algorithms, map tiling, or navigation data pipelines
